title: Short-lived versus static credentials
description: Dynamically issued short-lived credentials limit the damage window of a leak, while static long-lived keys remain exploitable until manually revoked.
body: Use when choosing how an application authenticates to a dependency. Do not default to a static long-lived key when dynamic issuance is available. A secret manager can mint short-lived credentials that expire automatically, so a leaked one is useless within minutes, whereas a static key stays valid until someone notices and revokes it. Inspect whether the platform supports dynamic credentials. Validation signals include credentials carrying a short expiry and auto-renewal. Risk boundary: long-lived static keys turn any exposure into an extended breach, so prefer short-lived issuance and escalate before introducing a new static key.
